meta.title: 0.5-containers deep audit (second pass)
meta.mandate: surface undetected regressions (stale callers, hard-to-detect issues only found in real testing); build test suites; report survives compaction; fixes for 0.5-introduced regressions go on the branch, everything else filed for 0.6 after beroset's review
meta.hardware_rules: lenok (1-2.3.3:1) STAYS OFF (weak battery); skipjack on 1-2:1 is the sanctioned live watch; prod 0.4 web service untouched
meta.status: COMPLETE — branch tip b1a0a1c, 7 audit commits, 94 tests

findings.what: ops.py imported uhubctl_cycle unused (leftover from 0.4 lazy-import change)
_background_warmer lived in webapp; serve-backend imported the frontend module, breaking ARCHITECTURE.md's stated seam rationale
findings.status: FIXED 2c9ddb3 — moved to ops (simplifies documented seams 2->1); docs updated

findings.what: webapp load_config went dead during the warmer move — caught by the NEW static test on its first run (proof of test value)
split mode: /api/status returns {ok:false,error:'backend unreachable'} when backend is down — the browser render() expects .hubs and would freeze; needs a 'backend unreachable' banner in the UI
serve-backend died with raw traceback on occupied port (EADDRINUSE)
findings.status: FIXED on branch (friendly message like serve)

findings.what: WorkbenchOp end/finally cuts the port WITHOUT graceful poweroff (unlike drain/charge via _end_port) — 'End checkout' leaves the watch RUNNING ON BATTERY, silently draining, while the UI shows the port off. Discovered live: skipjack was battery-running invisible after workbench stop. Fix candidate (0.6, needs mo's behavior decision): workbench finally should _end_port like drain
`uptime` COMMAND prints garbage on skipjack ('up 109 days'); /proc/uptime is sane — the uptime-verifies-reboot ground truth must use /proc/uptime
unexplained cold boot of skipjack ~4min before poweroff experiment (during onboard PPPS window) — possible VBUS-transition reboot quirk on TicWatch C2+; watch for recurrence

mass-poweroff false-confirm night: tonight proven that (a) endpoint fields are honest, (b) solo delivered-halt REALLY halts skipjack (37s time-to-adb + /proc/uptime 21.7s = cold boot), (c) adb_shutdown:false happens when a prior op (e.g. workbench end, F8!) left the watch battery-running off-adb. The mass scenario (7x adb_shutdown:true yet watches stayed on) needs a MULTI-WATCH repro with per-watch /proc/uptime forensics — outside tonight's one-watch sanction
quadlet combined ReadOnly=true with default secret mount — podman cannot create /run/secrets on a read-only rootfs; the hardened frontend NEVER STARTED. Would have failed on beroset's first trial
findings.status: FIXED 14a7829 (secret type=mount into /tmp tmpfs; hardened container verified serving)

findings.what: host adb server unreachable from containers: adb binds 127.0.0.1; host.containers.internal refused (adb + raw TCP probes). Beroset's reviewed preference (host adb) is impossible without `adb -a` (LAN exposure, against the split's purpose). Backend now runs its own adb server; ADB_SERVER_SOCKET escape hatch documented. RELAY TO BEROSET — this contradicts his review answer with evidence
both containers ignored SIGTERM as PID 1 (no default dispositions) — every stop burned the 10s kill timeout

TokenGate shutdown + systemd Restart=on-failure = restart loop under sustained attack (each cycle logs + re-shuts). Acceptable (RestartSec throttles, unit visibly failing) but worth a note in beroset's trial

deferred_to_0.6: F6: frontend 'backend unreachable' banner (UI chokes on error envelope as status)
F8: workbench end leaves watch running on battery (no graceful poweroff, unlike drain/charge) — needs mo's behavior decision, then _end_port in the finally

F11: mass-poweroff repro protocol — power 4+ watches, mass poweroff, per-watch /proc/uptime forensics after re-power to separate halt-delivery vs halt-effectiveness vs battery-running-invisible (F8 may be a big part of the original observation)
F10: watch for recurrence of unexplained TicWatch C2+ cold boot on VBUS transitions
frontend-wiring-test-gap partially closed by test_webui.py; headless-browser click test still open
test_suites_added: test_webui.py (onclick handlers defined, getElementById targets exist, JS fetch paths hit routes, node --check) — each check validated against planted historical bugs
test_rpcops.py (frontend ops all registered, allow-list IS the documented contract, handler behavior w/ mocked hardware)
verified_ok: cache-bust parity: all 26 routes identical main vs 0.5
webtemplate byte-identical to main (hotfixes flowed correctly)
no stale references to moved names (_sse_*_gen, _QueueHandler) anywhere
name-resolution sweep clean (known nested-scope false positives only)
94 tests green after suite additions
tri-mode status equality: 0.5-mono == 0.5-split byte-exact; v0.4 diffs fully explained (cold PowerCache + prod in-memory drain residue); cache warms in <75s
CC stable fields identical across v0.4/mono/split on live skipjack
screenshot via split: real 65KB JPEG through base64/RPC
screen on->off, settime(Europe/Berlin), notify, buzz, wifi toggle round-trip (state restored) all green over RPC
onboard stream survives client disconnect at 8s; op completed server-side (config updated, PPPS ran)
split-mode kill/resume: backend killed mid-workbench -> clean error envelope -> restart -> auto-resume -> stop -> 0 residue
12+12 concurrent status requests: all 200, both modes
TokenGate live: 41 rejections logged, escalating backoff (~19.5s/40), listener shutdown at threshold, process exit, no oracle on the wire
clean poweroff with adb alive = genuine halt (cold-boot proven via /proc/uptime)
teardown: fleet at rest, lenok untouched all night, prod v0.4 active throughout
hardened frontend container (read-only, cap-drop ALL, non-root, no volumes) serves after F12 fix
in-container adb server starts cleanly (rc=0, empty device list)
token rotation procedure end-to-end: rm secret -> new secret -> restart -> stack up on v2
container SIGTERM stop: 449ms after F14 fix
94 tests green at branch tip b1a0a1c
